rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 5, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Turner Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Mr. Granados was not "exposed to any Turner service, asbestos-containing or otherwise." Defendant only argues proper identification of defendant's employees. Defendant failed to sustain its burden because it did not demonstrate that the UCSF Medical Center job is the only site where plaintiffs and/or their witnesses identified defendant's employees working in the presence of Mr. Granados. For this reason, Willy Mautner's declaration is irrelevant and insufficient to sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that its employees were not present at any jobsite where Mr. Granados worked. Plaintiffs' argument that the stipulation for a shortened notice period has been withdrawn by their lawyer is rejected by the Court. No enforceable stipulation has been shown and even if the email correspondence is considered an enforceable stipulation, it contained no language demonstrating the intent of the parties as to whether the failure to comply with the terms of the agreement would have any consequences or penalties. Therefore defendant's motion is not denied on the basis that it failed to send plaintiffs a Word version of its Separate Statement at the time it served its moving papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
